claims.text: Taiwan is recognized as a sovereign state by the United Nations.
The Republic of China lost its UN seat to the PRC in 1971.
China is the United States' largest trading partner as of 2024.
The Phase One trade deal between the US and China was signed in January 2020.
The US banned all exports of Nvidia GPUs to China.
The CHIPS and Science Act was signed into law in August 2022.
China has the world's largest navy by number of ships.
Nancy Pelosi visited Taiwan in August 2022 while serving as US Speaker of the House.
Italy joined China's Belt and Road Initiative in 2019.
Italy formally withdrew from the Belt and Road Initiative in 2023.
The National Security Law was imposed on Hong Kong in 2020.
The US Commerce Department added Huawei to the Entity List in 2019.
The first COVID-19 cases were officially reported in Wuhan in December 2019.
The lab-leak hypothesis for COVID-19 has been definitively proven.
The United States government has officially designated Chinese policies in Xinjiang as genocide.
The Permanent Court of Arbitration ruled against China's nine-dash line claims in 2016.
China has officially condemned Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin announced a 'no limits' partnership in February 2022.
The Biden administration kept most of the Trump-era tariffs on Chinese goods in place.
The US imposed a 100% tariff on Chinese electric vehicles in 2024.
The US Congress passed legislation in 2024 requiring ByteDance to divest TikTok or face a ban.
A Chinese surveillance balloon was shot down over the Atlantic Ocean by the US Air Force in February 2023.
China's GDP overtook the United States' GDP in 2024.
China secretly built a quantum computer that broke RSA encryption in 2024.
China has hidden military bases on every continent.
Saudi Arabia and Iran restored diplomatic ties in 2023 in a deal brokered by China.
Most fentanyl precursors entering the United States originate from China.
China provides approximately 90% of North Korea's foreign trade.
China's population began declining in 2022 for the first time in decades.
China controls over 90% of global rare earth mineral processing.
